meaning: adj.殷勤的,高兴的,快活的,温文尔雅的
examples: Her father walked down the path, debonair in a three-piece suit, hat, and cane.
So who is the debonair looking guy in the photograph above?
He strolled about, look very debonair in his elegant new suit.
Cowardly over structural reforms, the negotiators were debonair when it came to cutting taxes.
In using the debonair, sophisticated man as the hero, the ad demonstrates that even smooth, desirable men use condoms.
